comment.body:
May’s CPI came in softer than expected—with headline up 0.1% MoM vs. 0.2%
In a positive S&P 500/10-year Treasury beta regime, such a “bad surprise” drives both stocks and yields lower.
gives Fed breathing room but stickier core and tariff pass-through argue against cuts before year-end.

comment.body:
I always struggle with these two:

Coding the centroid at 6039.94 - 6053.06 zone.

Coding 5993.93 for downside pivot here, we should start to see the downside gamma manifesitng, with downside target of 5968 especially during the PM. 

Do most people use the first note to trade off of or the second? My instincts say the first one makes more sense but not entirely what the second paragraph means.

comment.body: I had a thought.  Traditionally, CPI is more important than PPI.  But when tariffs start to hit in the numbers, It's producers paying tariffs for imported materials.  We might see tariff impact first in PPI.  This month?  IDK bc who knows what's real anymore with tariffs.
